title: Meta’s New Feel-Good AI Ad Uses a Song About the World Ending
excerpt: The clip features the David Bowie track “Five Years,” which includes lyrics such as “Earth was really dying (dying).”
content_html:
On Thursday morning, Meta published an ad to its official Instagram account with the message that artificial intelligence will not “make us less connected” or “leave us behind.” It was set to the 1972 David Bowie track “Five Years,” which is about, among other things, the end of the world.

“Call us optimists, call us dreamers, call us whatever the hell you want—but we’re betting on people, and we like those odds,” the narrator says, as a toddler plays with a butterfly, two parents laugh with their infant, and Jalen Brunson waves at the Knicks Championship Parade. (The ad also takes special care to highlight use cases for its controversial smart glasses that are not, say, harassing women while surreptitiously recording them.)

The snippet of “Five Years” used in the ad includes the following lyrics:

I heard telephones, opera house, favorite melodies

I saw boys, toys, electric irons and TVs

My brain hurt like a warehouse, it had no room to spare

I had to cram so many things to store everything in there

And all the fat, skinny people

And all the tall, short people

And all the nobody people

And all the somebody people

I never thought I'd need so many people

Perhaps understandably, it skips other parts of the song, including the opening:

News had just come over
We had five years left to cry in
News guy wept and told us
Earth was really dying

When reached for comment, Meta spokesperson Francis Brennan said he “would like to highlight what David Bowie himself said about ‘Five Years’ and its meaning: having optimism for the future.” Brennan sent along a 1972 quote about the song that he sourced to BowieBible.com.

“People are so incredibly serious and scared of the future that I would wish to turn the feeling the other way, into a wave of optimism,” reads the quote, which is itself sourced from NME Magazine. “If one can take the micky out of the future, and what it is going to be like … It’s going to be unbelievably technological.”

Nevertheless, BowieBible.com also notes that the song “speaks of a dystopian nightmare in which Earth enters its final five years, for reasons undisclosed, and the ensuing panic, violence, and attempts at redemption.”

BowieBible.com also quotes from a 2018 interview with drummer Mick Woodmansey: “Well, we chatted about it, and we knew it was about the end of the world and pretty depressing,” Woodmansey said of the song. “I remember when we recorded it, he was actually in tears. He was doing the vocal and he was actually crying.”

According to Jeremy D. Larson, the deputy director of Condé Nast sister publication Pitchfork, taking songs out of context for commercials is a time-honored tradition. He points to a Carnival Cruise ad that uses an Iggy Pop song about heroin addiction. (He also says that since Warner Chappell Music owns Bowie’s catalog, they are the ones with discretion over where his songs end up.)

“Meta choosing this specific song where the context for human connection is the apocalypse seems either really ignorant or really insidious,” Larson tells WIRED over Slack. “I don't know, maybe in that context it's genius.”

“Hearing [Bowie’s] work used for AI just feels especially and specifically nonconsensual,” he adds. (CEO Mark Zuckerberg, in a post about the ad, wrote that the company was “focused on giving every person the tools to reach your full potential and making sure the benefits of technology are distributed to everyone.”)

Over the past few years, tech companies have made several heavy-handed attempts to assuage people’s fears about their technology making the world worse. Just a few weeks ago, Anthropic released its own lofty ad, “There’s hope in hard questions.” It opens with a clip of a burning building and asks, “Who’s gonna hit the brakes if we need to?” before flashing over dozens of graves. The tone of the ad gradually shifts toward optimism, and ends with vague questions like, “What if we started to be more human again?” over images of families and a humpback whale.

In 2024, Google released a baffling ad about a father teaching his daughter, an aspiring athlete, how to hurdle. The father decides that he should use Gemini to draft a letter, from the perspective of his daughter, so that she can send it to decorated hurdler Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone. And during the 2022 Super Bowl, FTX aired a now-notorious ad starring Larry David, playing various Larry-esque characters, dismissing useful inventions throughout history—the toilet! the fork! the lightbulb!—before ultimately dismissing FTX.

“Don't be like Larry,” reads the text overlay at the end of the ad. “Don't miss out on crypto, on NFTs, on the next big thing.”

FTX filed for bankruptcy about nine months later.

Of course, it’s understandable that tech companies would use every tool at their disposal to increase goodwill. But the ads also demonstrate that many tech companies are failing to sell products that independently convince people that they are not actively making the world worse.

sentences.zh: 周四上午，Meta 在其官方 Instagram 账户上发布了一则广告，传达的信息是人工智能不会“让我们的联系变得更少”或“让我们落后”。

sentences.en: It was set to the 1972 David Bowie track “Five Years,” which is about, among other things, the end of the world.
sentences.zh: 歌曲以 1972 年大卫·鲍伊 (David Bowie) 的歌曲《五年》为背景，该歌曲的主题之一是世界末日。

blocks.html_en: “Call us optimists, call us dreamers, call us whatever the hell you want—but we’re betting on people, and we like those odds,” the narrator says, as a toddler plays with a butterfly, two parents laugh with their infant, and Jalen Brunson waves at the Knicks Championship Parade. (The ad also takes special care to highlight use cases for its controversial smart glasses that are not, say, harassing women while surreptitiously recording them.)

blocks.html_en:

I saw boys, toys, electric irons and TVs

My brain hurt like a warehouse, it had no room to spare

And all the tall, short people

And all the nobody people

And all the somebody people

I never thought I'd need so many people

sentences.zh: 我听到电话，歌剧院，最喜欢的旋律我看到男孩，玩具，电熨斗和电视我的大脑像仓库一样疼痛，它没有多余的空间我不得不塞进这么多东西来存放所有东西还有所有的胖子，瘦子和所有高个子，矮个子和所有无名之辈和所有有名之人我从来没有想过我需要这么多人

sentences.zh: 当联系到 Meta 发言人弗朗西斯·布伦南 (Francis Brennan) 发表评论时，他“想强调大卫·鲍伊 (David Bowie) 本人所说的‘五年’及其含义：对未来保持乐观。”

sentences.en: Brennan sent along a 1972 quote about the song that he sourced to BowieBible.com.
sentences.zh: 布伦南 (Brennan) 向 BowieBible.com 发送了有关他在 1972 年找到的这首歌的引言。

blocks.html_en: “People are so incredibly serious and scared of the future that I would wish to turn the feeling the other way, into a wave of optimism,” reads the quote, which is itself sourced from NME Magazine. “If one can take the micky out of the future, and what it is going to be like … It’s going to be unbelievably technological.”

sentences.en: “People are so incredibly serious and scared of the future that I would wish to turn the feeling the other way, into a wave of optimism,” reads the quote, which is itself sourced from NME Magazine.
sentences.zh: “人们对未来如此严肃和恐惧，我希望将这种感觉转变为一股乐观情绪，”这句话本身来自《NME》杂志。

sentences.en: “If one can take the micky out of the future, and what it is going to be like …
sentences.zh: “如果有人能把米奇从未来中剔除，那将会是什么样子……

sentences.en: It’s going to be unbelievably technological.”
sentences.zh: 这将是令人难以置信的技术。”

blocks.html_en: Nevertheless, BowieBible.com also notes that the song “speaks of a dystopian nightmare in which Earth enters its final five years, for reasons undisclosed, and the ensuing panic, violence, and attempts at redemption.”

sentences.en: Nevertheless, BowieBible.com also notes that the song “speaks of a dystopian nightmare in which Earth enters its final five years, for reasons undisclosed, and the ensuing panic, violence, and attempts at redemption.”
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，BowieBible.com 还指出，这首歌“讲述了一场反乌托邦噩梦，地球进入最后五年，原因未公开，以及随之而来的恐慌、暴力和救赎尝试。”

blocks.html_en: BowieBible.com also quotes from a 2018 interview with drummer Mick Woodmansey: “Well, we chatted about it, and we knew it was about the end of the world and pretty depressing,” Woodmansey said of the song. “I remember when we recorded it, he was actually in tears. He was doing the vocal and he was actually crying.”

sentences.en: BowieBible.com also quotes from a 2018 interview with drummer Mick Woodmansey: “Well, we chatted about it, and we knew it was about the end of the world and pretty depressing,” Woodmansey said of the song.
sentences.zh: BowieBible.com 还引用了 2018 年对鼓手米克·伍德曼西 (Mick Woodmansey) 的采访：“好吧，我们聊过这件事，我们知道这是关于世界末日的，而且非常令人沮丧，”伍德曼西谈到这首歌时说道。

sentences.en: “I remember when we recorded it, he was actually in tears.
sentences.zh: “我记得当我们录制这段视频时，他实际上泪流满面。

sentences.en: He was doing the vocal and he was actually crying.”
sentences.zh: 他在唱歌，实际上他在哭。”

(He also says that since Warner Chappell Music owns Bowie’s catalog, they are the ones with discretion over where his songs end up.)

sentences.en: He points to a Carnival Cruise ad that uses an Iggy Pop song about heroin addiction.

sentences.en: “I don't know, maybe in that context it's genius.”
sentences.zh: “我不知道，也许在这种情况下它是天才。”

sentences.en: over images of families and a humpback whale.

The father decides that he should use Gemini to draft a letter, from the perspective of his daughter, so that she can send it to decorated hurdler Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone. sentences.en: In 2024, Google released a baffling ad about a father teaching his daughter, an aspiring athlete, how to hurdle.
sentences.zh: 2024 年，谷歌发布了一则令人费解的广告，内容是一位父亲教他的女儿（一名有抱负的运动员）如何跨栏。

sentences.en: The father decides that he should use Gemini to draft a letter, from the perspective of his daughter, so that she can send it to decorated hurdler Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone.
sentences.zh: 父亲决定让双子座从女儿的角度起草一封信，以便她可以将其寄给获得荣誉的跨栏运动员西德尼·麦克劳克林-莱夫罗内。

sentences.en: And during the 2022 Super Bowl, FTX aired a now-notorious ad starring Larry David, playing various Larry-esque characters, dismissing useful inventions throughout history—the toilet!
sentences.zh: 在 2022 年超级碗期间，FTX 播放了一个现在臭名昭著的广告，由拉里·大卫 (Larry David) 主演，扮演各种拉里式的角色，对历史上有用的发明——厕所！

sentences.en: the fork!
sentences.zh: 叉子！

sentences.en: the lightbulb!—before ultimately dismissing FTX.
sentences.zh: 灯泡！——在最终解雇 FTX 之前。

blocks.html_en: “Don't be like Larry,” reads the text overlay at the end of the ad. “Don't miss out on crypto, on NFTs, on the next big thing.”

sentences.en: “Don't be like Larry,” reads the text overlay at the end of the ad.
sentences.zh: “不要像拉里一样，”广告末尾的文字叠加写道。

sentences.en: “Don't miss out on crypto, on NFTs, on the next big thing.”
sentences.zh: “不要错过加密货币、NFT 和下一件大事。”

blocks.html_en: FTX filed for bankruptcy about nine months later.

sentences.en: FTX filed for bankruptcy about nine months later.
sentences.zh: 大约九个月后，FTX 申请破产。

blocks.html_en: Of course, it’s understandable that tech companies would use every tool at their disposal to increase goodwill. But the ads also demonstrate that many tech companies are failing to sell products that independently convince people that they are not actively making the world worse.

sentences.en: Of course, it’s understandable that tech companies would use every tool at their disposal to increase goodwill.
sentences.zh: 当然，科技公司会使用一切可以使用的工具来增加商誉，这是可以理解的。

sentences.en: But the ads also demonstrate that many tech companies are failing to sell products that independently convince people that they are not actively making the world worse.
sentences.zh: 但这些广告也表明，许多科技公司销售的产品未能独立地让人们相信它们并没有积极让世界变得更糟。

content_html_zh:
周四上午，Meta 在其官方 Instagram 账户上发布了一则广告，传达的信息是人工智能不会“让我们的联系变得更少”或“让我们落后”。 歌曲以 1972 年大卫·鲍伊 (David Bowie) 的歌曲《五年》为背景，该歌曲的主题之一是世界末日。

“你可以称我们为乐观主义者，也可以称我们为梦想家，随便你怎么称呼我们——但我们赌的是人，我们喜欢这样的赔率。”旁白说道。此时，一个小孩在玩蝴蝶，两个父母和他们的婴儿一起欢笑，贾​​伦·布伦森在尼克斯队冠军游行中挥手致意。 （该广告还特别注意强调其备受争议的智能眼镜的用例，例如，这些眼镜不会在偷偷录制女性的同时骚扰她们。）

广告中使用的“五年”片段包括以下歌词：

我听到电话，歌剧院，最喜欢的旋律我看到男孩，玩具，电熨斗和电视我的大脑像仓库一样疼痛，它没有多余的空间我不得不塞进这么多东西来存放所有东西还有所有的胖子，瘦子和所有高个子，矮个子和所有无名之辈和所有有名之人我从来没有想过我需要这么多人

也许可以理解的是，它跳过了歌曲的其他部分，包括开头：

新闻刚刚传来，我们还有五年的时间去哭泣新闻人哭着告诉我们地球真的快要死了

当联系到 Meta 发言人弗朗西斯·布伦南 (Francis Brennan) 发表评论时，他“想强调大卫·鲍伊 (David Bowie) 本人所说的‘五年’及其含义：对未来保持乐观。” 布伦南 (Brennan) 向 BowieBible.com 发送了有关他在 1972 年找到的这首歌的引言。

“人们对未来如此严肃和恐惧，我希望将这种感觉转变为一股乐观情绪，”这句话本身来自《NME》杂志。 “如果有人能把米奇从未来中剔除，那将会是什么样子…… 这将是令人难以置信的技术。”

尽管如此，BowieBible.com 还指出，这首歌“讲述了一场反乌托邦噩梦，地球进入最后五年，原因未公开，以及随之而来的恐慌、暴力和救赎尝试。”

BowieBible.com 还引用了 2018 年对鼓手米克·伍德曼西 (Mick Woodmansey) 的采访：“好吧，我们聊过这件事，我们知道这是关于世界末日的，而且非常令人沮丧，”伍德曼西谈到这首歌时说道。 “我记得当我们录制这段视频时，他实际上泪流满面。 他在唱歌，实际上他在哭。”

据杰里米·D. 拉尔森是康泰纳仕集团姊妹刊物 Pitchfork 的副总监，断章取义地为广告拍摄歌曲是一个由来已久的传统。 他指出嘉年华邮轮的广告使用了一首关于海洛因成瘾的伊基流行歌曲。 （他还表示，由于华纳查佩尔音乐公司拥有鲍伊的歌曲目录，因此他们对鲍伊的歌曲最终去向拥有决定权。）

“Meta 选择这首特定的歌曲，其中人类联系的背景是世界末日，这似乎要么真的很无知，要么真的很阴险，”拉尔森通过 Slack 告诉《连线》杂志。 “我不知道，也许在这种情况下它是天才。”

“听到 [Bowie] 的作品被用于人工智能，我感觉特别特别不合情理，”他补充道。 （首席执行官马克·扎克伯格在一篇关于该广告的帖子中写道，该公司“致力于为每个人提供充分发挥潜力的工具，并确保每个人都能享受到技术带来的好处。”）

在过去的几年里，科技公司多次采取严厉措施来缓解人们对他们的技术让世界变得更糟的担忧。 就在几周前，Anthropic 发布了自己的崇高广告：“难题中蕴藏着希望。” 它以一座燃烧的建筑物的片段开始，并问道：“如果我们需要的话，谁会踩刹车？” 然后闪过数十座坟墓。 广告的基调逐渐转向乐观，并以模糊的问题结束，例如“如果我们再次变得更加人性化怎么办？” 家庭和座头鲸的图像。

2024 年，谷歌发布了一则令人费解的广告，内容是一位父亲教他的女儿（一名有抱负的运动员）如何跨栏。 父亲决定让双子座从女儿的角度起草一封信，以便她可以将其寄给获得荣誉的跨栏运动员西德尼·麦克劳克林-莱夫罗内。 在 2022 年超级碗期间，FTX 播放了一个现在臭名昭著的广告，由拉里·大卫 (Larry David) 主演，扮演各种拉里式的角色，对历史上有用的发明——厕所！ 叉子！ 灯泡！——在最终解雇 FTX 之前。

“不要像拉里一样，”广告末尾的文字叠加写道。 “不要错过加密货币、NFT 和下一件大事。”

大约九个月后，FTX 申请破产。

当然，科技公司会使用一切可以使用的工具来增加商誉，这是可以理解的。 但这些广告也表明，许多科技公司销售的产品未能独立地让人们相信它们并没有积极让世界变得更糟。
